diagrams.title: Basic Tree Structure
diagrams.description: A simple tree with 6 vertices showing root, internal nodes, and leaves
elements: Root at top, leaves (D,E,F) at bottom, internal nodes (B,C) in middle
Edges connect parent to children
Shows degree concept: leaves have degree 1
Vertices: 6, Edges: 5 (n-1 property)
diagrams.purpose: Understand tree components: root, internal nodes, leaves, parent-child relationships

diagrams.title: Tree Center Finding Process
diagrams.description: Step-by-step leaf removal to find tree center
elements: Original tree with labeled leaves
After 1st removal: leaves removed
After 2nd removal: remaining vertices highlighted
Shows how 1 or 2 center vertices emerge
diagrams.purpose: Visualize algorithm for finding center of a tree through iterative leaf removal

diagrams.title: Tree Traversals
diagrams.description: Same binary tree with nodes showing all three traversal orders
elements: Binary tree with root, left and right subtrees
Pre-order sequence displayed
In-order sequence displayed
Post-order sequence displayed
diagrams.purpose: Compare different tree traversal methods and their visiting orders

diagrams.title: Spanning Tree Example
diagrams.description: Original graph and its spanning tree
elements: Cyclic graph with 5 vertices and 7 edges
Spanning tree with same 5 vertices but only 4 edges
Shows which edges are removed to eliminate cycles
Highlights maximal acyclic subgraph property
diagrams.purpose: Understand spanning tree concept and how to obtain it by removing edges

diagrams.title: Binary Tree Types
diagrams.description: Different types of binary trees for comparison
elements: Full binary tree: every node has 0 or 2 children
Perfect binary tree: all internal nodes have 2 children, all leaves at same level
Complete binary tree: all levels filled except possibly last, nodes left-aligned
Skewed binary tree: every node has only one child
diagrams.purpose: Differentiate between full, perfect, complete, and skewed binary trees
